Cpl: Follows accepted military written communication guidelines (e.g., formats, principles, practices) as directed by chain of command (CoC)
Communicates their objectives verbally
Capt: Addresses groups verbally in a variety of formal and informal settings using appropriate verbal and non-verbal (e.g., appropriate body language, eye contact) communication skills
BGen: Extracts and communicates information relevant to goals and objectives in joint, intergovernmental, and multi-national activities
Col: Communicates messages with clarity, and impact, with or without preparation

Cpl: Uses appropriate means to communicate information and ideas (e.g., email, face-to-face)
MCpl: Adapts communication style to the audience to facilitate understanding of the message
Sgt: Communicates relevant information to meet the needs of the audience
WO: Tailors communication to a broad range of audiences and situations to increase acceptance, gain trust, and ensure understanding of the message
MWO: Identifies appropriate communication strategies to actively engage diverse internal audiences, while promoting the values and objectives of the CAF/DND
LCol: Tailors communication style and techniques to a broad range of audiences and situations to increase acceptance, gain trust, and ensure understanding of the message
BGen: Recognizes the need to shape, rather than change, a message for maximum effectiveness
CWO: Identifies appropriate communication strategies to actively engage diverse internal and external audiences, while promoting the values and objectives of the CAF/DND
MGen: Employs a flexible communication style to engage and influence external strategic partners (e.g., recognizes a dominant approach may not be appropriate in dealing with external agencies)

Provides consistent and clear messages that instill a sense of senior leader commitment and authenticity, that resonates with the intended audience by using language, examples, nuances, and symbols that are meaningful and
recognizable

Capt: Concurrently uses and attends to both verbal and non-verbal cues, as appropriate, to enhance understanding of messages
WO: Is sensitive to tone and nuances in messages being delivered
Major: Identifies and extracts relevant information from others as is appropriate for the situation
Col: Attends to and analyzes the overt and subtle information contained in messages, while considering the context
CWO: Uses both verbal and non-verbal cues to enhance understanding of messages, while considering the context

MCpl: Uses active listening and appropriate questioning to confirm own understanding, as appropriatei
Sgt: Confirms that the receiver understood the message by using well-directed questions, as appropriate
Capt: Seeks to ensure the correct message has been received, as appropriate
WO: Follows up to ensure the audience has understood the message and acted on or responded to the message(e.g., “presence on the shop floor”)
Major: Ensures that messages sent and received are well understood, as appropriate
MWO: Confirms that subordinates, peers, and supervisors understood the message through a variety of techniques (e.g., observation, direct questioning, having subordinate repeat message)
LCol: Uses well-directed questions or other techniques to ensure accuracy and understanding of messages being transmitted and received, as appropriate
BGen: Verifies comprehension of complex messages to ensure organizational success
CWO: Confirms comprehension of complex messages by subordinate leaders to ensure organizational success
MGen: Facilitates shared goals by creating a climate conducive to frank exchanges, and leverages dialogue with CAF/DND personnel and partners at the national-strategic and political levels to reinforce and provide feedback on Chief of
Defence Staff direction, policies, and new initiatives

MCpl: Keeps superiors, peers, and subordinates informed on progress and overall goals
Sgt: Establishes open lines of communication with key players in area of responsibility (AOR)
Major: Identifies and exchanges essential information with others in a timely manner
MWO: Communicates openly and regularly with superiors, subordinates, and partners
LCol: Establishes an atmosphere of communication where subordinates and stakeholders can freely express and exchange their ideas, views, and concerns
BGen: Communicates early, openly, and regularly with superiors, subordinates, and stakeholders
Col: Develops and maintains effective communication or information sharing mechanisms to promote organizational and cultural awareness
MGen: Creates a safe environment for candid and transparent exchanges of information to build trust, support, and commitment by communicating the strategic message and issues associated with operational directions

Cpl: Assists teammates with their tasks upon completion of own tasks
Sgt: Encourages team members to share the workload
Capt: Demonstrates and applies an understanding of group dynamics and team building to facilitate collaboration among team members
WO: Works together with others by sharing information, knowledge, and expertise to move forward on goals
Encourages team members to share the workload
Major: Works toward collaborative solutions within and between teams
MWO: Coordinates and promotes collaborative efforts and the sharing of resources and expertise across the organization
LCol: Publically acknowledges and values team members’ and subordinate teams’ collaborative efforts
BGen: Creates an environment for team effectiveness, where strengths are known and built upon and learning is shared to build capacity
Col: Creates and maintains collaborative relationships within and between teams by sharing knowledge, building consensus towards objectives, and creating collective goals
MGen: Fosters a robust team attitude that is collaborative and collegial with CAF/DND and external counterparts (e.g., Other Government Departments [OGDs], foreign military counterparts, academia, industry)

Cpl: Encourages teammates to accomplish team tasks
Sgt: Provides teams with the support necessary to achieve their tasks
MWO: Encourages and motivates teams to work towards unit goals
BGen: Synchronizes team’s work within and between various higher levels (e.g., commands) and agencies
Col: Fosters enthusiasm and common purpose within own immediate team and across their organization
CWO: Fosters enthusiasm and common purpose across teams to attain goals
MGen: Maintains team motivation by providing sound advice, making timely decisions, responding promptly to concerns, and sharing lessons learned from within and outside the command

MCpl: Integrates new members into the team quickly
BGen: Promotes sharing of expertise among teams to achieve superior service or results within CAF/DND and for stakeholders and clients
CWO: Cultivates commitment and generates momentum of the team/group to achieve strategic priorities
MGen: Integrates individual and collective capabilities by creating solid and mutually supporting teams that support the achievement of organizational, institutional, and GoC objectives

MCpl: Promotes team cohesion
Sgt: Fosters team spirit and promotes the importance of teamwork
Capt: Nurtures team cohesion and morale and promotes the importance of teamwork to the team members and others
Major: Is aware of their influence on team morale and adjusts their behaviour accordingly
MWO: Fosters team spirit and promotes the importance of teamwork across and within sub-units
LCol: Is aware of their influence on team morale and adjusts their behaviour accordingly
BGen: Directs and promotes the importance of team morale within the organization and/or the CAF/DND
Col: Is aware of their influence on team morale and adjusts their behaviour accordingly
CWO: Fosters team spirit and promotes the importance of teamwork across the CAF/DND (e.g., beyond own unit and/or AOR)
MGen: Creates and maintains team morale within their organization as a whole, ensuring inter-unit morale

Cpl: Keeps teammates informed to facilitate collaborative team efforts
MCpl: Encourages the sharing of ideas and information, and communicates frequently within the team by setting an example
Capt: Encourages all team members to share ideas and communicate as necessary, to achieve objectives
WO: Creates a climate of transparency by encouraging input from all team members
Major: Promotes open communication and cooperation among team
LCol: Communicates relevant and new information regularly to team members to ensure a climate of transparency
BGen: Communicates recent advances and priorities, while maintaining their accessibility, to ensure a climate of transparency

Cpl: Demonstrates self-assurance when accomplishing assigned tasks
Inspires the confidence of peers
MCpl: Inspires the confidence of subordinates and peers
Capt: Inspires the confidence of superiors
WO: Inspires the confidence of others both within and external to their organization
Major: Demonstrates confidence and optimism when leading teams
MWO: Leads with self-assurance and competence at the organizational level
LCol: Maintains and promotes professional and personal credibility
BGen: Demonstrates great presence and self-assurance carrying out their functions and when representing the institution to national and international groups, the Canadian public, and the media
Nurtures the confidence of others based on their knowledge and expertise
Col: Demonstrates self-assurance and competence in leading their organization
CWO: Inspires confidence of others, both internal and external to the CAF/DND
MGen: Recognizes the importance of personal and professional credibility, and actively strives to foster, improve, and maintain credibility at all levels internal and external to CAF/DND

LCol: Assumes responsibility for outcomes of unit projects/missions
BGen: Readily accepts responsibility for their decisions and actions
Col: Is responsible for the decisions/programs/ processes within or across the organization, even when unpopular
CWO: Assumes responsibility for organizational decisions, programs, and processes
MGen: Demonstrates pan-CAF responsibility and institutional leadership by ensuring that accountabilities are integrated into organizational policies, practices, and commitments
Sets the example by accepting responsibility for all decisions and actions while fostering the same behaviour in others

BGen: Fosters an environment throughout the CAF/DND that encourages and supports independent functioning
MGen: Cultivates an environment that enables decisive and independent action in the face of challenges, changes, and uncertainty

MCpl: Motivates and guides others to achieve goals
Sgt: Encourages involvement and commitment to unit objectives and activities by constantly setting an example
Major: Inspires others to work towards unit objectives
BGen: Inspires CAF/DND members by giving meaning to the work being accomplished
CWO: Inspires others to achieve CAF/DND objectives
MGen: Inspires with intellectual leadership (i.e., leading the intellectual agenda by producing and communicating new, or recycling old, knowledge) in support of successful CAF operations, DND capabilities, and GoC objectives

Cpl: Provides credible advice using facts and/or experience
MCpl: Convinces subordinates and peers to support their initiatives
Sgt: Uses credibility and convincing arguments to achieve objectives
Capt: Uses credibility to influence others to achieve objectives
WO: Influences others to reach compromises to achieve objectives
MWO: Uses their reputation and sound reasoning to gain support for their initiatives
LCol: Uses credibility, sound reasoning, and persuasion to gain support for unit initiatives

Sgt: Influences team functioning with their actions
Capt: Takes actions that have a positive impact on team objectives
WO: Takes actions that have a positive impact beyond own AOR
Major: Positively impacts unit functioning and objectives
MWO: Influences unit objectives and functions
BGen: Positively impacts CAF/DND, whole-of-government (WoG), and/or element objectives
Col: Positively impacts multiple organizational objectives
MGen: Identifies and gains the support of key actors/decision-influencers to advance CAF goals

Cpl: Demonstrates CAF military ethos on and off duty
MCpl: Demonstrates professionalism on and off duty, setting a positive example
Sgt: Promotes and maintains adherence to CAF values and of military professionalism
WO: Reinforces the importance of professional military standards within the unit
Major: Reinforces the importance of military professional standards
MWO: Reinforces the importance of professional military standards within the organization
LCol: Demonstrates CAF military ethos and professionalism on and off duty
BGen: Promotes the values and role of the military in a constitutional democracy to internal and external audiences within the national, international, public, and political domains
Col: Models and builds a culture for military professionalism
CWO: Exemplifies and promotes military professionalism to internal and external audiences
MGen: As an Institutional leader, understands that the military profession needs to evolve to keep pace with societal norms, while ensuring that organizational effectiveness is maintained

Capt: Supports and serves as an example by acting in ways that are in keeping with CAF military and Canadian values, and accepted CAF military customs and traditions
Major: Contributes to the establishment of accepted CAF military customs, values, and traditions
MWO: Upholds and shapes the evolution of the military ethos
BGen: Instils a sense of pride in, and displays, CAF military customs, values, and traditions
CWO: Instils a sense of pride in CAF military customs, values, and traditions
MGen: Promotes doctrine, concepts, symbols, traditions, and rituals through deliberate role modelling and consistent communication with networks both internal and external to the CAF to ensure they reflect the ever-evolving Canadian
culture

Cpl: Demonstrates familiarity with the professional precept of duty before self
MCpl: Takes actions and makes decisions that are in keeping with the professional precept of duty before self
Sgt: Instills in subordinates the culture of duty before self
Capt: Takes actions and makes decisions in keeping with the professional precept of duty before self, and demands the same of others
WO: Supports an environment that reinforces the professional precept of duty before self
LCol: Ensures, enables, and educates on the professional precept of duty before self
BGen: Inculcates the duty-before-self philosophy in senior CAF leaders
Col: Fosters an organizational culture of duty before self

Cpl: Obeys lawful orders and directives
MCpl: Adheres to a clearly defined CoC and obeys the code of conduct
Sgt: Enforces immediate obedience to lawful commands
Capt: Adheres to a clearly defined CoC and obeys the code of conduct
WO: Maintains a climate of obedience to the code of conduct
Major: Enforces lawful orders and directives
MWO: Establishes the appropriate group norms on military conduct and enforces military discipline
BGen: Adheres to a clearly defined CoC and obeys the code of conduct
Col: Adheres to a clearly defined CoC and obeys the code of conduct
CWO: Champions an environment that promotes the core values in the code of conduct
MGen: Conveys the importance and expectation of adherence to the code of conduct and military law while issuing clear warnings about the consequences of non-compliance

LCol: Displays and models courage when facing risks and obstacles
BGen: Displays and models courage in their convictions and in the face of monumental challenges
MGen: Combines clarity of purpose with personal conviction and a sense of determination to be the best role model they can be

WO: Allocates scarce resources in a balanced and transparent way
MWO: Coordinates and manages resources to ensure the practical needs of the organization are met, and goals are achieved
LCol: Identifies, allocates, and prioritizes the use of needed resources for multiple projects, in-line with higher objectives
Col: Forecasts resource requirements (i.e., human, financial, and material) to ensure optimal organizational performance
MGen: Has an in-depth understanding of the strategic contexts within which current fiscal policy, procedures, practices, systems, and accountability or authority exist

Major: Monitors and ensures the appropriate, effective, and efficient use of resources and assets, and prioritizes conflicting resource demands
BGen: Develops and implements processes and infrastructures to optimize the efficient and transparent usage of CAF/DND resources and assets

Major: Identifies inefficiencies and explores ways of effectively utilizing resources
LCol: Identifies and corrects ineffective and/or inefficient use of resources and assets
BGen: Establishes rigorous accountability systems and performance indicators to assess and optimize organizational policies, structures, and overall performance
Col: Develops strategies for subordinates to address resource gaps/issues
MGen: Examines CAF/DND structure and organizational capabilities to identify gaps and prepare resources pan-CAF (e.g., workforce) to meet current and future needs

MWO: Identifies and corrects wasteful practices to optimize resources
CWO: Monitors, prioritizes, and relocates conflicting resources and assets to ensure objectives are achieved

MCpl: Uses knowledge of subordinates (e.g., their strengths, weaknesses, personality) to develop effective teams
Sgt: Sets attainable goals that balance unit needs with the capabilities of team members
Capt: Balances, when possible, the operational requirements and needs of the CAF/DND with subordinates’ personal needs
LCol: Balances, when possible, the operational requirements and needs of the CAF/DND with subordinates’ personal needs
BGen: Balances, when possible, the operational requirements and needs of the CAF/DND with subordinates’ personal needs
CWO: Understands and effectively uses diverse teams across the CAF/DND
MGen: Utilizes strategic workforce planning to optimize the employment of CAF personnel

MCpl: Identifies impediments, and sets priorities and courses of action (COAs) for subordinates to meet work-related goals
BGen: Demonstrates purposeful, decisive, consultative, and adaptive leadership
MGen: Champions personnel by monitoring CAF members’ well-being, Human Resource structures, and policies, ensuring members have the capacity to perform effectively now and in the future
WO: Minimizes the negative impact of decisions on others, while ensuring that objectives are met

Sgt: Distributes tasks among subordinates, while considering priorities, available resources, and subordinates’ skills
Capt: Delegates appropriately, and holds subordinate leaders accountable for agreed upon commitments
WO: Delegates appropriately, balancing the needs of subordinates with the needs of the unit

MCpl: Monitors progress and quality of subordinates’ work, making timely and appropriate adjustments as required
Sgt: Monitors progress and quality of teams’ work to accomplish unit goals
Capt: Monitors progress and quality of subordinates’ objectives, and demands subordinates make timely and appropriate adjustments as required
Major: Monitors progress and quality of teams’ work, making timely and appropriate adjustments as required
MWO: Ensures that activities, programs, and processes are monitored
BGen: Oversees a range of significant programs/projects and time-sensitive issues using appropriate resources both internal and external to the CAF/DND
Col: Ensures that activities, programs, and processes are monitored and that their efficacy is assessed
CWO: Oversees a range of significant programs/projects and time-sensitive issues using appropriate resources both internal and external to the CAF/DND
MGen: Places a high priority on ensuring that effective mechanisms and practices are in place to facilitate feedback loops and monitor efficacy

BGen: Ensures formal organizational structures are in place to recognize teams’ and members’ contributions across the CAF/DND
MGen: Actively recognizes the contributions of individuals and teams through award programs, personal and public recognition, and professional development opportunities

MCpl: Process (OPP) Demonstrates awareness of and has had some exposure to OPP
Sgt: Contributes to the planning and implementation of goals and objectives at the tactical and operational level
Capt: Develops, directs, and contributes to the execution of operational level goals, objectives, and plans
Process (OPP) Demonstrates the capacity to generate military COAs within a team-based analytical process
WO: Contributes to the development of strategic guidance and supports strategic planning
Major: Contributes to the planning and implementation of goals, and objectives at the tactical, operational, and/or strategic level
LCol: Contributes to the development of strategic guidance and directs strategic planning
BGen: Process (OPP) Demonstrates the capacity to contribute to national strategy formulation and has mastered OPP
Future Requirements Conducts visionary analyzes to compare current directions with future aspirations
Col: Masters strategy, strategy formation and strategic planning
MGen: Interprets the operational environment and translates this into the Commander’s Intent so that subordinate leaders can achieve the mission
OR

When in Command, applies GoC/CAF/DND policy, Higher Headquarters direction, and interprets the operational environment to establish the Commander’s Intent so that subordinate leaders can achieve the mission

MCpl: Sets priorities and develops a plan of action to meet subordinates’ goals
Identifies obstacles for own and subordinates’ objectives and, when possible, overcomes them
Sgt: Prioritizes and plans work to meet teams’ requirements
Provides recommendations for overcoming team obstacles
WO: Prioritizes and plans work to meet unit requirements, adjusting plans when necessary
Major: Anticipates obstacles and prepares contingency plans to ensure goals are met
MWO: Prioritizes and plans work to meet organizational requirements
Anticipates obstacles and prepares contingency plans to ensure organizational goals are met
BGen: Develops effective strategic plans that balance competing demands across the many structures of the CAF/DND
Col: Plans for and maintains military capability required to meet overall CAF objectives
CWO: Prioritizes and plans work to meet the CAF/DND’s requirements
Ensures that contingency plans are in place for unforeseen events
MGen: Develops strategies and approaches to take advantage of opportunities, support allies, address barriers, and mitigate risks (where possible)

Capt: Implements strategies to achieve tactical objectives
WO: Implements strategies to ensure tactical success
Major: Implements strategies to achieve operational efficiencies
MWO: Coordinates activities and personnel to ensure unit objectives are achieved
LCol: Ensures organizational structures and policies are in place to meet objectives
Future Requirements Anticipates future requirements of their unit and takes appropriate action
Col: Ensures wide-span coordination of activities within and outside their organization to reach objectives
CWO: Ensures structures and support are aligned to meet organizational objectives
MGen: Aligns the system(s) to set the conditions for mission success by monitoring system(s) requirements and outcomes in the context of government regulations and adjusting the system’s parameters (e.g., governing policies, allocated
resources)
Future Requirements Enforces the requirements of government policies, highlights shortfalls in policies, advocates for adjustments to policies, and creates contingency plans for an uncertain future

Cpl: Performs job tasks reliably, skillfully, and safely
MCpl: Performs tasks specific to AOR and occupation
Sgt: Masters occupational technical skills and administrative responsibilities
Capt: Accomplishes complex tasks in AOR and occupation
Major: Masters occupational technical skills and administrative responsibilities
MWO: Excels at occupation and AOR duties, often expanding their AOR to assist with organizational goals

Cpl: Acquires and applies technical capabilities relevant to employment
MCpl: Refines and applies knowledge, skills, abilities, and qualifications relevant to employment and occupation
Capt: Demonstrates knowledge, skills, abilities, qualifications, and experience relevant to employment and occupation at the tactical level
WO: Demonstrates mastery of technical knowledge and skills including disciplinary and personnel issues
Major: Demonstrates expert knowledge, skills, and abilities relevant to employment (e.g., job, environment, AOR) at the operational level
MWO: Demonstrates knowledge beyond occupation to include the mastery of disciplinary and personnel issues
LCol: Demonstrates broad knowledge, skills, and abilities in a variety of domains and contexts of employment, typically at the joint level

Sgt: Monitors changes in own occupation to maintain technical currency
Provides technical advice to others to ensure enhanced quality of work outputs
Capt: Provides sound advice to CoC based on technical expertise
WO: Disseminates and exchanges technological knowledge, procedures, and best practices with CoC and subordinates
Major: Provides technical advice both up and down the CoC to ensure enhanced quality of work outputs and to resolve areas of concern
MWO: Provides sound advice to CoC based on technical expertise
LCol: Shares and exchanges technological and professional views, procedures, best practices, and implementation thereof with CoC

Cpl: Applies technically relevant regulations, orders, directives, and standard operating procedures (SOPs)
MCpl: Enforces relevant regulations, orders, policies, and directives
WO: Contributes to the development of technical standards, concepts, and procedures
LCol: Demonstrates expertise in interpreting and applying relevant regulations, orders and directives

Cpl: Initiates actions required to complete their tasks
MCpl: Undertakes tasks that go beyond routine demands without prompting
Sgt: Takes initiative by volunteering or accepting to take on unfamiliar or additional tasks
WO: Identifies and acts on issues to enhance subordinates’ and unit objectives
Major: Invests extra effort into the achievement of results proactively, when required
LCol: Accepts increased responsibilities and proactively leads their organization
BGen: Takes the initiative to create new opportunities, goals, and strategies for their organization and/or the CAF/DND
Col: Displays initiative by proactively providing superiors with solutions to problems and the capability to execute the solution
CWO: Promotes a climate of initiative within the CAF/DND
MGen: Champions initiatives with significant potential paybacks, taking into consideration possible adverse consequences, based on an assessment of the risks, benefits, and impacts, in alignment with CAF/DND strategic objectives

MCpl: Recognizes and advises CoC about problem areas
Sgt: Identifies and acts on issues instead of waiting for direction or hoping for them to resolve themselves
Capt: Identifies and acts on issues and problems in AOR instead of waiting or hoping for them to solve themselves
WO: Seeks opportunities and takes the initiative to advance organizational objectives
Major: Identifies and acts on issues and problems external to AOR
LCol: Identifies and/or acts on issues or opportunities to enhance unit outcomes, or to advance unit goals
BGen: Identifies and/or acts on issues or opportunities to enhance CAF/DND goals and objectives
CWO: Identifies and acts on opportunities to enhance CAF/DND outcomes
MGen: Focuses on longer-range organizational objectives despite daily obstacles and emergencies

Cpl: Is determined and remains task-focused regardless of the working conditions
Sgt: Works hard at achieving difficult and long-term objectives
Capt: Works hard towards achieving difficult objectives
MWO: Perseveres towards challenging organizational objectives despite conditions and additional demands
BGen: Strives towards achieving longer term organizational objectives despite competing objectives
Col: Keeps a clear focus on longer range organizational objectives despite daily obstacles and unforeseen events
CWO: Focuses on longer range CAF/DND objectives despite daily obstacles and unforeseen events
MGen: Shows determination and commitment to achieve long-term/strategic objectives and implement decisions and directives, regardless of challenges and adversities

Cpl: Takes appropriate and timely action
MCpl: Keeps CoC informed of the risks associated with a course of action
Capt: Acts without hesitation when action is required and bases the action on informed risks
WO: Anticipates and assesses risks and identifies ways to mitigate them
Major: Acts without hesitation and with purpose while assessing and accepting the risks
MWO: Shifts priorities to respond quickly to emerging opportunities and risks
Understands risk management and takes risks to support higher CI
LCol: Anticipates, identifies, assesses, and accepts risks, and identifies ways to mitigate them
Col: Encourages responsible risk taking to achieve organizational objectives
MGen: Accepts and functions within a constant state of risk, accepting that there will never be a 100% solution and that the CAF must always strive to achieve institutional excellence to the extent possible

MCpl: Promotes transparent and honest behaviours in subordinates
Ensures subordinates act with integrity at all times
Sgt: Shows transparency and consistency in the way they manage, and makes decisions when appropriate
Capt: Promotes transparent and honest behaviours in subordinates
WO: Ensures integrity in unit practices
Major: Ensures subordinate leaders are promoting, emulating, and monitoring ethical behaviours of their subordinates
MWO: Promotes an ethical climate by ensuring expectations for ethical behaviour are understood and fosters a reprisal free environment
Promotes integrity in unit practices, programs, and policies
LCol: Ensures integrity in organizational practices
BGen: Tolerates no ethical failings of subordinate leaders regardless of rank or position
Col: Displays courage of conviction by taking efforts to maintain high ethical standards throughout the organization
CWO: Fosters an ethical climate by holding self and subordinate leaders to high ethical standards
MGen: Leads the organization in promoting and practicing ethical behaviours by initiating, personifying, and leading new CAF/DND programmes (e.g., ethics, harassment, OP HONOUR, The Path)

Displays moral potency, which comprises moral courage, moral efficacy (i.e., the confidence to act morally), and moral ownership (i.e., a sense of ownership over the moral aspects of their environment)

Sgt: Considers the moral and ethical implications of different courses of action and makes appropriate choices despite positive or negative implications or collateral damage
Capt: Seeks to understand the moral and ethical implications of different courses of action before determining the best course of action
WO: Makes the right decision when faced with ethical dilemmas
Major: Makes the appropriate decisions when faced with ethical dilemmas even when it requires challenging the CoC or causes collateral damage
MWO: Demonstrates the moral courage to make the best decision when faced with morally conflicting options
LCol: Makes fair, equitable, and appropriate decisions guided by moral reasoning
BGen: Acts as a Steward of the profession protecting, sustaining, and promoting ethical decision-making practices
Makes the appropriate decisions when faced with ethical dilemmas even when it requires challenging the CoC or causes collateral damage
Col: Makes the appropriate decisions when faced with ethical dilemmas even when it requires challenging the CoC or causes collateral damage
CWO: Stewards the Profession of Arms by promoting ethical decision-making practices
MGen: Acts as a model of how to take decisive action when addressing ethical decisions

MCpl: Identifies unethical conduct and takes appropriate action and forwards concerns to superiors
Sgt: Identifies unethical situations and takes timely action to address them
Capt: Identifies unethical conduct or conflicting interests within unit, takes immediate appropriate action, and forwards concerns to superiors
WO: Takes steps to prevent unethical conduct
Major: Identifies potential ethical dilemmas and conflicting interests and takes steps to avoid and prevent them, seeking guidance if necessary
LCol: Assesses workplace to identify ethical risks and takes timely action to address them (e.g., develops and implements communication and action plans)
BGen: Identifies and avoids unethical behaviours, while recognizing that the ambiguity of correct action increases with rank
Col: Identifies and resolves conflicting interests that have the potential to become ethical issues/risks
CWO: Fosters a climate that encourages others to identify unethical conduct and take required action
MGen: Demonstrates values-based leadership, understanding that effectiveness means paying attention to all values and dealing with competing values, and managing the risks inherent in making choices and decisions

Cpl: Makes a conscious effort to build rapport with others
MCpl: Encourages relationship building among subordinates
Capt: Develops and maintains interpersonal relationships that contribute to mission success
WO: Facilitates the development of effective relationships and encourages effective interactions
Major: Creates an environment that fosters positive interpersonal relationships
LCol: Relates with others (e.g., subordinates, superiors, peers and stakeholders) to better understand who they are, and their needs and challenges
BGen: Creates and maintains bonds of collegiality and mutual respect with their counterparts in other government departments (OGDs) through conveying not only an understanding of their contribution to the community and nation but
also the important roles their civilian colleagues play in national security and defence

MCpl: Fosters two-way trust in dealing with subordinates
Sgt: Creates and maintains a climate of trust with supervisors
Capt: Fosters two-way trust in dealing with others
WO: Creates and maintains a climate of trust with the CoC
Major: Creates and maintains a climate of trust and confidence within the team
MWO: Creates and maintains a climate of trust within CAF/DND
BGen: Espouses trust of followers and uses creditability to gain the trust of others at all levels within and external to the CAF
CWO: Assists subordinate leaders with creating and maintaining a climate of trust within CAF/DND
MGen: Establishes and promotes trust between the CAF and Canadian society at the national, provincial, and local levels

MCpl: Demonstrates genuine concern for team members and their families
Sgt: Represents the concerns of subordinates to superiors, where appropriate
Col: Leads by fostering an environment of genuine concern for members and their families

Cpl: Treats others with respect
MWO: Models a climate of respect for others
MGen: Builds and promotes a bilingual, inclusive, and healthy organization that is respectful of the diversity of all people and their skills and contributions, free from harassment and discrimination

Sgt: Adapts interaction style and tactics to respond to a range of individuals with diverse backgrounds and perspectives, and encourages subordinates to do the same
Capt: Adapts interaction style to respond appropriately to a range of individuals with diverse background, perspectives, and opinions
WO: Adopts the leadership style that is most effective for the context
Major: Adapts interaction style to respond appropriately to a range of individuals with diverse background, perspectives, and opinions and encourages subordinates to do the same
MWO: Adapts appropriately to individual sensitivities, backgrounds, and cultures within the CAF/DND
LCol: Considers others’ perspectives while recognizing that others may not be able to consider their perspective
BGen: Maintains a diplomatic, tactful, and understanding demeanour when engaging with external partners, representatives from foreign countries or the media
Col: Reacts appropriately to the sensitivities, backgrounds, cultures, and subcultures within and outside the organization to advance objectives and build trust
CWO: Adapts appropriately to the sensitivities, backgrounds, and cultures within and outside the CAF/DND
MGen: Develops and implements strategic action plans, policies, and procedures that support diversity and cultural responsiveness within the organization

Cpl: Attempts to resolve conflicts, and when required, seeks guidance from the CoC
LCol: Monitors group climate and/or anticipates conflicts and acts to prevent them from occurring or escalating, and/or mitigate their effects
BGen: Ensures that interpersonal conflict within the CAF/DND is addressed in a timely manner and that senior members focus on generating constructive solutions rather than emphasizing problems
Col: Ensures that effective conflict resolution processes are in place and understood by subordinates
CWO: Uses diplomacy and negotiation techniques to resolve conflicts across the CAF/DND in an effective manner
MGen: Ensures comprehensive programs are implemented and maintained that promote dialogue between groups to help them understand the multiple causes or factors that gave rise to their disagreement

Cpl: Maintains composure when faced with stressful situations
Performs effectively in stressful situations
MCpl: Conveys positive outlook to others while maintaining composure in sustained periods of stress
Maintains perspective on and juggles multiple objectives during periods of stress
Sgt: Assists others to remain positive, productive, and composed in stressful situations, while doing the same
Capt: Assists others to remain positive, productive, and composed in stressful situations
WO: Keeps mission objectives in perspective during stressful situations and encourages subordinates to do the same
Major: Responds appropriately to others in stressful situations, helping them to remain positive, productive, and calm
Keeps mission objectives and immediate concerns in perspective, when faced with a stressful situation
BGen: Energizes and inspires others during times of elevated stress or in threatening situations by remaining calm, steady, decisive, and effective
CWO: Energizes and inspires personnel during times of elevated stress
MGen: Retains focus, maintains composure, and achieves objectives during high profile events (e.g., whether public, international, multi-military, pan-governmental, or during operations)

MCpl: Applies effective coping strategies to prevent stress-related problems
Sgt: Creates and maintains a healthy lifestyle (e.g., healthy behaviours)
Capt: Recognizes potentially stressful situations and applies effective coping strategies to prevent stress-related problems
WO: Creates and promotes a balanced lifestyle (e.g., work/life balance, healthy behaviours)
MWO: Ensures subordinates are aware of successful strategies for coping with stress and maintaining a balanced lifestyle
BGen: Provides support and mechanisms for subordinate leaders to maintain focus on organizational objectives in stressful or dangerous situations
Col: Encourages subordinates to remain focused on organizational objectives in stressful or dangerous situations
CWO: Personifies and models active and effective coping strategies and a balanced lifestyle
LCol: Encourages and facilitates dialogue on stress and successful strategies for coping
MGen: Creates and monitors the efficacy of pan-CAF policies and services that support positive stress management, and seeks regular feedback from care service providers regarding the health of the organization

Sgt: Identifies workplace stressors, communicates them to the CoC, and reduces them when possible
Capt: Buffers stress and pressures, so that subordinates can focus on their tasks and pre-emptively adopts mitigation strategies
WO: Anticipates and recognizes workplace stressors and seeks ways to minimize their negative effects (e.g., by providing timely communications)
Major: Anticipates and recognizes stressful situations and seeks ways to minimize their negative effects on subordinates, others, and self
MWO: Mitigates workplace stress and pressures from superiors so that subordinates can focus on their tasks, and advises superiors of such stressors
LCol: Monitors the level of stress in the environment and takes steps to help others maintain effectiveness (e.g., secures additional assistance)
BGen: Directs policy changes to facilitate stress level reduction where applicable
Col: Identifies tolerance levels for workplace stress within the unit and reduces stressful factors where possible
CWO: Develops formal strategies to mitigate stress and pressures arising from the CoC so that subordinates can focus on their tasks
MGen: Implements and ensures the use of sound strategies and best practices to assist others in coping with stressful or dangerous situations

Cpl: Demonstrates basic knowledge of coping mechanisms and of the effects of stress on well-being
MWO: Encourages and facilitates dialogue between self and others on workplace stress and coping strategies
LCol: Facilitates an environment of understanding of stress effects, coping mechanisms and CAF resources supporting mental health
Col: Ensures an environment of understanding of stress effects, coping mechanisms and CAF resources supporting mental health
MGen: Promotes healthy living and stress reduction programs, and encourages the development of measures to educate personnel on stress reduction strategies

MCpl: Demonstrates critical thinking and logical reasoning skills
Capt: Employs critical thinking skills and logical reasoning in developing and executing effective COAs and solutions
WO: Employs critical thinking skills and logical reasoning in developing solutions and providing recommendations
Major: Engages in inductive and deductive reasoning while focusing on relevant information, resulting in consistently effective solutions
MWO: Employs critical thinking skills and logical reasoning when formulating plans, determining priorities, weighing alternative solutions, and considering risks
LCol: Analyzes information and weighs alternative strategies for dealing with current and future problems and situations, taking into account risks, benefits, and implications
BGen: Employs critical thinking skills and logical reasoning when formulating effective plans and priorities with strategic intent, weighing alternative solutions and considering inherent risks while creating clarity
Col: Employs appropriate analytical thought processes to resolve issues and assess risk, while considering the appropriate timeframe(s) (e.g., short-, medium-, long-term
CWO: Employs critical thinking skills when dealing with current and future problems and situations, taking into account risks, benefits, and implications, and demands the same of subordinates
MGen: Demonstrates a strategic perspective by analyzing wider issues, broader implications, balancing short- and long-term considerations, and identifying opportunities and threats

MCpl: Establishes priorities, compares alternative solutions, and analyzes risks to achieve success
Sgt: Gathers and analyzes relevant information before developing and implementing practical solutions to problems
Establishes priorities, compares alternative solutions, and analyzes risks to achieve success
Capt: Draws on a variety of sources including knowledge and experience of self and others when considering COAs or solutions
Establishes priorities, compares alternative solutions, and analyzes risks to achieve mission success
WO: Establishes priorities, compares alternative solutions, and analyzes risks to achieve success
Major: Considers and balances multiple components of a situation to offer recommendations
Identifies and analyzes key elements of issues in order to create effective solutions despite limited time and incomplete information
MWO: Establishes priorities, compares alternative solutions, and analyzes risks to achieve success
LCol: Analyzes and integrates information from a variety of sources, extracting key facts while dealing with dynamic situations in order to create effective solutions
BGen: Routinely promotes the reasoned use of intuition/insights obtained through experience, and informal and formal professional development, to deal with complex, non-linear issues and/or problems
Col: Identifies and considers second and third order consequences when deriving final solutions/decisions
CWO: Extracts, integrates, and analyzes information relevant to goals and objectives in joint, intergovernmental, and/or multi-national activities
MGen: Identifies, analyzes, and interprets complex GoC and key stakeholders’ critical issues and assumptions about the future
Evaluates strategies for optimal organizational objectives and pan-CAF goals

MCpl: Makes decisions based on training, experience, and analysis of risks
Sgt: Makes recommendations for COAs even when faced with limited information
MWO: Exhibits decisiveness under tight deadlines, making decisions on the basis of their analysis

CWO: Identifies and considers all relevant factors, stakeholders, priorities, organizations, and their relationships when making decisions

WO: Recognizes some of the organization’s systems and how problems in one system may effect another system (i.e., starts to develop systems thinking)

LCol: Provides regular and timely constructive feedback to subordinate leaders on progress and areas for improvement

Cpl: Demonstrates a positive attitude when faced with change
MCpl: Implements change coming from the CoC and mitigates resistance
Sgt: Initiates change within AOR, when it benefits the team, unit, and/or organization

WO: Analyzes the effectiveness of change initiatives and recognizes when change is not required

Creates momentum for organizational change and uses change management skills necessary to implement it
Communication Engages stakeholders in the change process to increase their endorsement
MGen: Spearheads the development and implementation of change strategies, when necessary, by developing or adjusting systems and processes to facilitate change across the CAF/DND, even with limited support
Manages change through appropriate contingency planning, which builds on lessons learned through previous change experiences, and manages associated risks
Communication Empowers people to embrace and contribute to change in their own dynamic and creative ways

Creates support for an innovative environment by ensuring the flow of knowledge between organizations, environments, and levels (e.g., creates knowledge networks), and by supporting the generation of innovative action and results for

Capt: Demonstrates the link between own work activities and the Commander’s Intent (CI)
WO: Understands how their role and role of subordinates fits with the Commander's Intent
Major: Maintains up-to-date knowledge of the CAF structures, processes, objectives and key players relevant to own AOR
Demonstrates the link between own work activities and the Commander’s Intent (CI)
MWO: Understands how their role and role of subordinates fits with the Commander's Intent
LCol: Demonstrates the link between own work activities and the Commander’s Intent (CI)

Understands the relationships between the component parts of the CAF (people, culture, processes, structures, and technology) and major alliances/allies affecting Canadian security (i.e., NORAD/US Military/NATO), and views them as
interrelated pieces of a larger system that operate in the broader context of federal government policy, Canadian societal interests, and global security concerns
Sgt: Demonstrates an understanding of the structures, processes, and key players relevant to team(s)’ AOR
Understands how their role and role of subordinates fits with the CI

Capt: Uses understanding of structures, processes, and key players to accomplish own and team tasks
WO: Aligns organizational goals and strategies with their teams’ work
Major: Applies own awareness of the organization’s current and emerging priorities and key players to make recommendations, gain cooperation and support, and achieve unit objectives
MWO: Anticipates and influences outcomes based on an understanding of the status, culture, and current climate of the CAF/DND
LCol: Leverages the inner workings of CAF’s multiple formal and informal systems and headquarters to gain support for, synchronize, and achieve organizational objectives
BGen: Anticipates issues and opportunities from multiple sources and positions within the organization to address and capitalize on them, while recognizing constraints (e.g., policy, public works, Treasury Board, industry Canada)
Col: Monitors and uses their understanding of power relationships and dynamic tensions among key players and issues to develop strategies and positions at the community, national, and international levels
CWO: Uses knowledge of the CAF/DND to coordinate, achieve, and gain support for CAF/DND objectives
MGen: Applies vast organizational knowledge to integrate activities across organizations, functional areas, and the CAF
Keeps current with trends and issues affecting the CAF/DND, partner organizations, and the GoC, and contributes to adapting CAF/DND strategy accordingly

LCol: Pursues knowledge of their unit in relation to the greater CAF, DND, and the WoG
BGen: Demands subordinate leaders develop deeper knowledge of the organization in relation to the greater CAF, DND, and the WoG

Capt: Demonstrates an understanding of the environment in which the unit operates
WO: Demonstrates an understanding of the general environment in which the organization operates (e.g., political, social, economic, cultural, historical contexts)

CWO: Demonstrates a broad understanding of interagency, multinational, and public issues relevant to the CAF/DND

Aligns CAF-wide systems and structures with the vision, to ensure implementation success

Seeks and maximizes opportunities to promote Canada and the CAF’s global engagement strategy
Partnership Development Creates and facilitates interpersonal connections to develop and enhance partnerships, alliances, and networks that advance shared interests and the needs of the CAF/DND

Synchronizes military capabilities with OGDs and agencies
Col: Translates, communicates, and promotes the vision within and outside their organization to build commitment to the strategic mission
Provides strategic direction and aligns initiatives across systems with the CAF’s future directions

Partnership Development Identifies, negotiates, and cultivates relationships with internal and external organizations at the community, provincial, national, and international levels to advance defence objectives

CWO: Promotes, translates, and communicates the vision to others within and/or outside the CAF/DND to build commitment to it

MGen: Champions the vision, facilitates its implementation, and ensures the vision’s success
Contributes to rethinking the CAF/DND vision by considering current and emerging national and international political realities
Partnering Fostering Partnerships Fosters personal and institutional networks to engage external partners and move the CAF forward in the future security environment

Partnership Development Develops the professional networks necessary to work as part of a joint or combined team, and forms collaborative relationships with other militaries and government departments
Engages with government in all its dimensions, Canadian society at large, allies, and a wide variety of international and non-governmental organizations

Partnership Alignment Consults and collaborates with national agencies and international allies to coordinate and integrate all sources of power and influence into the national security strategy for the security of Canadians
